plainEnglish: When a keyboard user tabs to an element, there must be a visible focus indicator showing where they are. Don't remove the browser's default focus outline (outline: none) without providing a clearly visible replacement, or keyboard users get lost.
whyItMatters: Keyboard users can't tell which element is focused—and so can't navigate at all—when the focus indicator is removed or invisible.
quickCheck: Tab through the page and confirm every focused element shows a clearly visible outline or highlight; search your CSS for 'outline: none' or 'outline: 0'.
commonMistakes: Setting outline: none (or 0) on :focus with no visible replacement style.
A focus style so faint or low-contrast it's hard to see.
Applying focus styles only for mouse interaction, or relying solely on :hover.
Custom controls that never receive a visible focus state.

codeExamples.label: Focus ring removed with no replacement
codeExamples.code:
/* Keyboard users can no longer see where focus is */
.btn:focus { outline: none; }
